name: Beaked Hazelnut
summary: The cool-forest hazelnut — more characteristic of the true Northern Hardwood Forest understory than the warmer American hazelnut already on the list. Named for the long bristly husk that encloses each nut.
notes: Corylus genus supports ~130+ Lepidoptera species. Hard mast feeds grouse, turkeys, jays, squirrels, and chipmunks. Multi-stemmed 6–12 ft shrub forming open thickets. Yellow catkins in April provide early-spring pollen for native bees. Better cold-tolerance and shade-tolerance than American hazelnut.

planting.soil: Acidic to neutral; well-drained; tolerates rocky ground
planting.site_notes: Choose beaked hazelnut over American hazelnut for upland Pocono Plateau plantings — better cold-and-shade match for NHF. Wire cage for the first 3–5 years; deer browse leaves and twigs heavily.
